commands.review_summary:
The branch changes model-call diagnostics to count high-frequency streamed text, thinking, and tool-call delta bytes from `delta`, updates the OTEL/logging descriptions, and adjusts focused regression expectations.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests -11, Docs +2. Total +9 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main serializes each snapshotless stream event for byte accounting, while the PR head counts delta strings directly. I did not reproduce a live long-running local-model starvation session.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer compatibility judgment for the exported diagnostics metric baseline, plus any desired exact-head validation, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(agents): count streamed model deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- Count streamed `text_delta`/`thinking_delta`/`toolcall_delta` diagnostics from their incremental `delta` bytes instead of serializing the full event.
- Drop growing `partial` snapshots from fallback stream byte accounting so diagnostics do not repeatedly walk the accumulated assistant message.
- Add regression coverage proving text-delta accounting does not serialize large partial snapshots and keeps streams alive if byte inspection throws.
- Document the bounded `responseStreamBytes` semantics for high-frequency streamed deltas and align OTEL docs/metric descriptions with that bounded payload contract.

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- Rebasing onto `origin/main` completed on head `1888fdf8cc5`; obsolete unrelated gate-unblocker commits were dropped because their patch contents were already upstream at that point.
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`: passed.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md docs/logging.md`: passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md docs/logging.md extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts`: passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts`: passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`: passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts --reporter=dot`: passed, 2 shards / 86 tests.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` on this head reached typecheck/core lint and then failed only on the unrelated current-main Microsoft Foundry lint drift: `provider=aws`, `lease=cbx_abd08bf126ba`, `slug=pearl-prawn`, `run=run_754a7a07054d`, exit 1, failed lane `lint extensions`, errors in `extensions/microsoft-foundry/provider.ts`.
- The unrelated Foundry lint blocker is fixed and exact-head validated in #88814: `provider=aws`, `lease=cbx_cb720cfb366a`, `slug=tidal-barnacle`, `run=run_84fbccfe9ad6`, `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: dense model streams no longer make model-call diagnostics serialize the full growing `partial` assistant message on every delta.

Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree after rebase; AWS Crabbox changed-gate proof was attempted and isolated an unrelated current-main Foundry lint blocker that is repaired in #88814.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md docs/logging.md`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label diagnostic-delta-rebased-check-changed --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests passed, including assertions that `text_delta` accounting does not call `partial.toJSON`, counts incremental `delta` bytes, and keeps stream delivery alive when byte inspection cannot read a chunk; the OTEL service test suite still passes with the updated response-byte metric description; logging and OpenTelemetry docs now describe the same bounded metric semantics.

Observed result after fix: `responseStreamBytes` is computed from incremental delta bytes for high-frequency stream deltas and from snapshotless chunks for other partial-bearing stream events, while OTEL metadata and docs describe the exported histogram as bounded streamed response payload bytes.

What was not tested: a live long-running local-model session; exact remote changed-gate success for this PR after #88814 lands.
